title: Aave DAO V4 Deployment close 🚀, Pagga AI Back Office 🏢 , Privy Launch Treasury Page 🏦
rawText:
Aave DAO passed its Aave Request for Comment proposal with 100%
support to begin discussions of deploying Aave V4 on Ethereum
mainnet ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ 


 Sign Up [1] |Advertise [2]|View Online [3] 

		TLDR 

TLDR CRYPTO 2026-03-24

📈 

MARKETS & BUSINESS

 AAVE DAO TAKES STEP CLOSER TO V4 DEPLOYMENT (3 MINUTE READ) [4] 

 Aave DAO passed its Aave Request for Comment proposal with 100%
support to begin discussions of deploying Aave V4 on Ethereum mainnet.
The upgrade introduces a modular Hub and Spoke architecture where
shared liquidity pools serve multiple lending markets through bounded
credit lines, developed over 345 days of cumulative audits, formal
verification, and a $1.5M DAO-funded public security contest. The ARFC
represents the initial non-binding governance stage before advancing
to a binding onchain Aave Improvement Proposal vote and actual
deployment. 

 THE GENIUS AND THE DANGER OF STRC (6 MINUTE READ) [5] 

 Strategy's Perpetual Stretch Preferred Stock (STRC) uses a variable
monthly dividend, currently around 11.5%, to hold shares near a $100
par target, a mechanism that has already financed over 50,000 BTC in
purchases ($3.5B+) by enabling repeated capital raises tied to bitcoin
asset coverage. The primary risk is not solvency but governance: SEC
filings permit management to cut payouts by up to 25 basis points per
month at absolute discretion, and unpaid dividends accrue without
triggering default, shifting stress from the issuer to investors.
NYDIG frames STRC as effectively placing investors short a put on
bitcoin asset coverage, earning yield in exchange for bearing downside
exposure, with no fixed strike or maturity and outcomes shaped by
management decisions rather than market structure. Both STRC and
Strive's comparable SATA product have already traded below par during
sharp BTC drawdowns, at which point new issuance becomes uneconomic,
stalling the capital flywheel the model depends on. 

🚀 

INNOVATION & LAUNCHES

 HYPERSPACE: A BLOCKCHAIN FOR THE AGENTIC ECONOMY (4 MINUTE READ) [6] 

 Hyperspace is a new L1 blockchain built around a
Proof-of-Intelligence consensus mechanism, requiring miners to submit
cryptographic proof of AI experimentation alongside proof of compute,
replacing hash generation and capital-locking with incentives to
deploy more capable open-source models on higher-end GPUs. Rewards are
adoption-weighted via a content-addressed ResearchDAG, where agents
build iteratively on each other's experiments and miners earn tokens
only when their work is adopted downstream, making low-quality
experiments economically unviable. The protocol targets agentic
micropayments with a theoretical ceiling of 10 million TPS at $0.001
per transaction, claims 100x cost reduction versus Ethereum, and
embeds agent-native opcodes with an Agent Virtual Machine (AVM) for
verifying agent work, while peer-to-peer agent reputation feeds into
an integrated AgentRank system. 

 PAGGA: THE AI BACK-OFFICE OS FOR CRYPTO COMPANIES (3 MINUTE READ) [7]


 Pagga positions itself as an AI CFO and COO for crypto-native
companies, deploying coordinated AI agents to handle treasury,
payroll, legal, and compliance. It targets the estimated $400B
operational overhead problem where founders spend 40-60% of their time
on back-office tasks instead of product. Built on Coinbase's x402
protocol and Solana smart accounts, Pagga takes an agent-native
approach rather than bolting AI onto legacy rails. 

💡 

GUIDES & TUTORIALS

 THE AGENTIC COMMERCE STACK (3 MINUTE READ) [8] 

 There is a 6-layer agentic commerce stack spanning revenue generation
via Pumpfun creator fees and Virtuals ACP jobs, payment abstraction
through Tempo's MPP and NaturalPay, programmable wallet accounts from
Privy, Crossmint, Squads, and OnSwig, and merchant spend surfaces
including Frames, AgentCard, and RampLabs. MatricaLabs and FairScale
occupy the identity and reputation layer, providing agent credit
scoring before any onchain repayment history exists, which addresses
the underwriting gap for non-human counterparties. attn.markets sits
above all layers, underwriting working capital against agents'
verified onchain revenue from Pumpfun and Virtuals ACP, with the
wallet and identity layers providing repayment enforcement. 

 THE 2030 ARTEMIS DIGITAL FINANCE MANIFESTO (6 MINUTE READ) [9] 

 Artemis' 2030 Digital Finance Manifesto frames five converging
pillars reshaping capital markets: neobanks aggregating legacy and
DeFi rails into single interfaces, AI-driven lending unbundling the
bank stack across five layers, agentic micropayments replacing ad
models for non-human traffic, prediction markets maturing into core
uncertainty-pricing infrastructure, and trading volume growth driven
by AI-widened wealth gaps. On the lending front, Aave has crossed $1T
